subject: Physics (WASSCE)
document_title: West African Senior School Certificate Examination Physics Syllabus
aims: Acquire proper understanding of the basic principles and applications of Physics
Develop scientific skills and attitudes as pre-requisites for further scientific activities
Recognize the usefulness, and limitations of scientific method to appreciate its applicability in other disciplines and in every life
Develop abilities, attitudes and skills that encourage efficient and safe practice
Develop scientific attitudes such as accuracy, precision, objectivity, integrity, initiative and inventiveness

acquisition_of_knowledge_and_understandi: Scientific phenomena, facts laws, definitions, concepts and theories
Scientific vocabulary, terminology and conventions (including symbols, quantities and units)
The use of scientific apparatus, including techniques of operation and aspects of safety
Scientific and technological applications with their social economic and environmental implications
information_handling_and_problem_solving: Locate select, organize and present information from a variety of sources including everyday experience
Analyse and evaluate information and other data
Use information to identify patterns, report trends and draw inferences
Present reasonable explanations for natural occurrences, patterns and relationships
experimental_and_problem_solving_techniq: Carry out experimental procedures using apparatus
Make and record observations, measurements and estimates with due regard to precision, accuracy and units
Interpret, evaluate and report on observations and experimental data
Identify problems, plan and carry out investigations, including the selection of techniques, apparatus, measuring devices and materials
Evaluate methods and suggest possible improvements
State and explain the necessary precautions taken in experiments to obtain accurate results

scheme_of_examination.paper_1: 50 multiple choice questions lasting 1 1/4 hours and carrying 50 marks
scheme_of_examination.paper_2: Two sections, Sections A and B lasting 1 1/2 hours and carrying 60 marks. Section A: seven short-structured questions, answer any five for 15 marks. Section B: five essay questions, answer any three for 45 marks
scheme_of_examination.paper_3: Practical test for school candidates or alternative to practical work paper for private candidates. Three questions, answer any two in 2 3/4 hours for 50 marks

part_1_interaction_of_matter_space_and_t.notes: Simple structure of matter, three physical states (solid, liquid, gas), particle nature evidence (Brownian motion, kinetic theory), pressure in a gas, evaporation/boiling, cohesion, adhesion, capillarity, crystalline vs amorphous substances

part_1_interaction_of_matter_space_and_t.topics: 2. Fundamental and derived quantities and units
part_1_interaction_of_matter_space_and_t.notes: Fundamental quantities (length, mass, time, electric current, luminous intensity, thermodynamic temperature, amount of substance) and units (m, kg, s, A, cd, K, mol). Derived quantities (volume, density, speed) and units (m^3, kgm^-3, ms^-1)
Position as location using X, Y, Z axes, measurement using string, metre rule, vernier calipers, micrometer screw gauge. Direction as bearing using compass and protractor. Distance vs displacement
Distinction using lever/beam/electronic balance for mass and spring balance for weight. Units: kg and N
Concept of time interval, measurement using heart-beat, sand-clock, ticker-timer, pendulum, stopwatch/clock. Unit: second(s)

Volume, density, relative density. Pressure in fluids, Pascal's principle, hydraulic press, car brakes, atmospheric pressure, barometers, manometers, siphons, syringes, pumps. Archimedes' principle and law of flotation

Types of motion (random, rectilinear, translational, rotational, circular, orbital, spin, oscillatory). Relative motion, cause of motion (forces: contact and non-contact/field forces, solid friction, viscosity/fluid friction, terminal velocity, circular motion, banking of roads)
Concept of speed and velocity, uniform/non-uniform, ticker-timer, distance/displacement-time graph, instantaneous speed/velocity
Concept of acceleration/deceleration, uniform/non-uniform, velocity-time graph, equations of motion with constant acceleration, motion under gravity
Scalars vs vectors, representation, addition of forces/vectors using force board, resolution of vectors, resultant velocity
Principle of moments, torque, couple, conditions for equilibrium of rigid bodies under parallel and non-parallel forces, centre of gravity and stability
Illustration, definition, speed, acceleration, period, frequency, amplitude, energy of S.H.M., forced vibration and resonance
First law (inertia), second law (force, acceleration, momentum, impulse, conservation of momentum), third law (action and reaction)

part_2_energy_mechanical_and_heat.notes: Forms of energy, world energy resources (renewable and non-renewable), conservation of energy

Concept of work, energy as capability to do work, work done in gravitational field, potential energy, kinetic energy, conservation of mechanical energy, power, application of mechanical energy in simple machines (levers, pulleys, inclined plane, wedge, screw, wheel and axle, gears, efficiency)

Temperature measurement, thermometric scales/liquids, effects of heat on matter, thermal expansion (linear, area, volume expansivities, consequences, anomalies), heat transfer (conduction, convection, radiation), gas laws (Boyle's, Charles', pressure, general gas law), measurement of heat energy (heat capacity, specific heat capacity), latent heat, specific latent heat, evaporation, boiling, vapour pressure, humidity, relative humidity, dew point

part_3_waves.notes: Mechanical waves, pulsating system, ripple tank, waveform, amplitude, wavelength, frequency, period, mathematical relationships (v = f lambda, T = 1 / f)
Transverse and longitudinal waves, mathematical representation of wave motion y = A sin(wt +/- 2 pi x / lambda)
Reflection, refraction, diffraction, interference, superposition, standing/stationary waves
Sources of light, rectilinear propagation, reflection at plane and curved surfaces, refraction at plane and curved surfaces, optical instruments, dispersion of white light
Types of radiation in electromagnetic spectrum (radio, infrared, visible light, ultra-violet, X-rays, gamma rays)

Sources, transmission, speed in media, echoes and reverberation, noise and music, characteristics (pitch, loudness, quality), vibration in strings, forced vibration, resonance, harmonics, overtones, vibration of air in pipes

part_4_fields.notes: Concept of fields (gravitational, electric, magnetic), properties of force fields
Acceleration due to gravity (g), Newton's law of gravitation, universal gravitational constant (G), gravitational potential, escape velocity

Electrostatics (production of charges, storage, lines of force, Coulomb's law, electric field intensity, electric potential, capacitance) and Current electricity (production from cells, Ohm's law, resistance, electric circuits, conduction, energy and power, shunt/multiplier, resistivity/conductivity, measurement)
Properties of magnets, magnetization/demagnetization, magnetic flux/density, magnetic force on current-carrying conductors and moving charges, electromagnets, earth's magnetic field
Electromagnetic induction, Faraday's law, Lenz's law, motor-generator effect, inductance, eddy currents, power transmission and distribution
Graphical representation of e.m.f. and current, peak and r.m.s. values, series circuit, reactance, impedance, vector diagrams, resonance, power in a.c. circuit

part_5_atomic_and_nuclear_physics.notes: Models of the atom (Thomson, Rutherford, Bohr, electron-cloud), energy quantization, photoelectric effect, thermionic emission, X-rays
Composition of the nucleus, radioactivity (natural and artificial), nuclear reactions (fusion and fission, binding energy, mass defect, E = delta mc^2)

harmonized_topics_for_short_structured_q: 4. Elastic properties of solid (Hooke's law, Young's modulus, thermal conductivity)
8. Electrical conduction through materials (Electronics, semiconductors, doping, junction diode)
9. Structure of matter (Diffusion via kinetic theory)
